author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
review: A dazzling portrait of the Jazz Age, The Great Gatsby follows the mysterious Jay Gatsby and his obsessive pursuit of the American Dream. Through Nick Carraway's eyes, Fitzgerald weaves a tale of wealth, illusion, and lost love set against the glittering backdrop of 1920s New York. A timeless meditation on ambition and the hollowness of excess.

author: Harper Lee
review: Told through the innocent eyes of young Scout Finch, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel confronts racial injustice in the American South. Her father, lawyer Atticus Finch, becomes a symbol of moral courage as he defends a Black man falsely accused of a crime. A powerful and moving story that remains as relevant today as ever.

author: George Orwell
review: In a terrifying totalitarian future, Winston Smith rebels against the all-seeing Party and its leader Big Brother. Orwell's chilling dystopia explores surveillance, propaganda, and the destruction of truth. A haunting and prophetic novel that feels alarmingly close to reality in the modern world.

author: J.D. Salinger
review: Holden Caulfield, a disillusioned teenager, wanders New York City after being expelled from prep school, raging against the phoniness of the adult world. Salinger's iconic coming-of-age novel captures adolescent angst and the painful transition to adulthood with raw honesty and dark humor.

author: Jane Austen
review: Elizabeth Bennet and the proud Mr. Darcy clash in this sparkling romantic comedy of manners. Austen masterfully dissects class, marriage, and social expectations in Regency England. Witty, warm, and endlessly quotable, Pride and Prejudice remains one of the greatest love stories ever written.

author: J.R.R. Tolkien
review: Bilbo Baggins, a comfort-loving hobbit, is swept into an epic quest to reclaim a dwarf kingdom from the dragon Smaug. Tolkien's beloved adventure is rich with magic, humor, and heart. A perfect introduction to Middle-earth that enchants readers of all ages with its warmth and wonder.

bookName: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
review: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard and enters the magical world of Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. Rowling's enchanting debut novel blends mystery, friendship, and adventure into an unforgettable story. The beginning of a generation-defining series that has captured hearts worldwide.

author: Paulo Coelho
review: Santiago, a young Andalusian shepherd, embarks on a journey across the desert in search of his Personal Legend. Coelho's philosophical fable is a meditation on dreams, destiny, and the courage to follow one's heart. A short but profoundly inspiring read that has touched millions of lives around the world.

author: Paula Hawkins
review: Rachel takes the same commuter train every morning and becomes obsessed with a seemingly perfect couple she sees from the window. When the woman goes missing, Rachel finds herself entangled in the investigation. A gripping psychological thriller packed with unreliable narrators, shocking twists, and dark secrets.

author: Aldous Huxley
review: Set in a futuristic World State where citizens are engineered and conditioned for happiness, Brave New World questions the price of stability and pleasure. Huxley's unsettling dystopia explores consumerism, technology, and the loss of individuality. A thought-provoking classic that grows more relevant with each passing decade.

author: Dan Brown
review: Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon is drawn into a deadly conspiracy involving secret societies, religious history, and a murder inside the Louvre. Brown's fast-paced thriller weaves art, history, and mystery into an unputdownable adventure. A global phenomenon that kept millions of readers guessing until the final page.

author: George Orwell
review: When the animals of Manor Farm overthrow their human farmer, they dream of a utopia built on equality. But as the pigs seize power, the revolution slowly corrupts. Orwell's brilliant political allegory is a sharp and timeless satire of totalitarianism, propaganda, and the abuse of power.

author: J.R.R. Tolkien
review: Frodo Baggins must carry the One Ring across the treacherous lands of Middle-earth to destroy it in the fires of Mount Doom. Tolkien's monumental epic is a breathtaking journey of friendship, sacrifice, and the struggle between good and evil. The cornerstone of modern fantasy literature.

author: Margaret Mitchell
review: Scarlett O'Hara, a willful Southern belle, struggles to survive the devastation of the American Civil War and Reconstruction era. Mitchell's sweeping historical romance is a vivid portrait of a vanishing world, filled with passion, resilience, and heartbreak. One of the best-selling novels of all time.

author: Suzanne Collins
review: In the dystopian nation of Panem, sixteen-year-old Katniss Everdeen volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised fight to the death. Collins crafts a gripping story of survival, rebellion, and media manipulation. A thrilling and emotionally charged novel that sparked one of the most beloved series in Young Adult fiction.

author: Herman Melville
review: Captain Ahab's all-consuming obsession with the white whale Moby Dick drives his crew to the edge of madness and destruction. Melville's monumental novel is a rich exploration of fate, revenge, and humanity's struggle against nature. A challenging but deeply rewarding masterpiece of American literature.

author: Khaled Hosseini
review: Amir, a privileged boy from Kabul, betrays his loyal servant Hassan and spends decades haunted by guilt. When a chance for redemption arises, he returns to a Taliban-ruled Afghanistan. Hosseini's debut novel is a devastating and ultimately hopeful story of friendship, betrayal, and the possibility of second chances.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
review: Raskolnikov, a destitute student in St. Petersburg, murders a pawnbroker and is consumed by guilt and paranoia. Dostoevsky's psychological masterpiece dives deep into the criminal mind, morality, and the search for redemption. A relentlessly intense novel that remains one of the greatest works in world literature.

author: James Dashner
review: Thomas wakes up with no memory inside a massive maze populated by other boys and deadly mechanical creatures. As he searches for an escape, dark secrets about their captivity begin to unravel. A pulse-pounding science fiction thriller full of mystery, action, and unexpected twists that will keep you reading late into the night.

author: John Green
review: Hazel Grace Lancaster, a sixteen-year-old cancer patient, meets the charming Augustus Waters at a support group and falls deeply in love. Green's heartbreaking yet beautiful novel explores mortality, love, and what it means to leave a mark on the world. A tearjerker that stays with you long after the final page.
